methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
PILGRIM, a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Jake Adler, is evaluated against a peer set of 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies. The broader comp universe spans 10 private and 2 public comparables, including Tenna Systems, Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

A peer-median approach was applied across the 5-company set, which ranges from $60M (Tenna Systems at Seed) to $29.0B (Scale AI at Series A). The median valuation across this cohort stands at $300M, reflecting the typical entry point for a company at PILGRIM's stage and sector. This method yields an implied valuation of $300M.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $255M to $345M, with a median of $300M. The wide dispersion in the peer set—particularly the $29.0B outlier—introduces material uncertainty around capital intensity and market opportunity in Defense & Security. The presence of only one Seed-stage comparable (Tenna Systems at $60M) suggests limited direct precedent for PILGRIM's exact positioning. Key uncertainties include competitive differentiation within the peer cohort, product-market validation metrics, and whether PILGRIM's technology warrants valuation closer to the Seed floor or toward the Series A median.
